pack_fact_check.title: fusion.ts sourceWeight applies vector penalty under hard signals

claims_checked.claim: File src/retrieval/fusion.ts exists
sourceWeight() function defined at line 141
Snippet body matches fusion.ts lines 141-153 verbatim

Snippet uses '...' ellipsis to truncate, omitting lines 155-164 which apply effectiveTaskItemTypeBoosts and labelConfidenceMultiplier — those are part of sourceWeight() and material to a full explanation

Pack implies sourceWeight starts from policy.sourceWeights[source]; in truth it uses effectiveSourceWeight() (line 143) which additively layers a task-profile delta on top — pack obscures the taskProfile contribution

pack_fact_check.title: DEFAULT_POLICY values for source weights and hard-signal penalties

claims_checked.claim: File src/retrieval/policy.ts exists
sourceWeights block at lines 184-194 matches snippet (worktree:1.3, metadata:1.15, graph:1.1, memory:1.08, lexical:1.0, vector:0.92)
hardSignalBoost sources/bonus and hardSignalVectorPenalty values at lines 195-196 match

Cited line 184 — that line is the opening of the sourceWeights block, but the cited content also includes lines 195-196 which are 11 lines away; the single line citation is misleading for a multi-block snippet

Pack omits taskProfiles block (lines 222-253) including the exploration profile that adds +0.04 to vector — relevant because the classified taskType for this query is 'exploration' and the actual effective vector weight under hard signals would be 0.92 + 0.04 - 0.08 = 0.88, not the 0.84 the child later asserts
File docs/FLOW_LOGIC.md exists
Cited line 173 — the actual 'Fusion:' header is on line 172; the body begins at line 174. Line 173 is the blank line after the header. Off-by-one or by-two depending on convention

Content quote stitches two non-adjacent paragraphs: 'Weighted below exact metadata and lexical matches when files, symbols, or errors are present' is from line 170 (Vector search section), NOT from the Fusion section. The pack presents synthesized/paraphrased text as if it were one block
Substance is consistent with code: docs do say metadata strongest and vector last
File src/components/paywall-selection-modal.tsx exists
Path does not exist anywhere in tuberosa repo (find returned nothing)
Item has zero relevance to fusion/policy/retrieval — pure topical drift

pack_fact_check.title: Old memory: 'vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa'

claims_checked.claim: Memory asserts vector is strongest signal
Cross-check against policy.ts line 184-194: vector=0.92 is the LOWEST weight, worktree=1.3 is the highest
Under hard signals (the very topic of the query) vector is additionally penalized by -0.08; opposite of memory's claim
Pack labels itemType 'memory' but provides no provenance, freshness timestamp, or supersession marker that should have triggered suppression

inter_pack_contradictions.claim_left: Vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa retrieval; metadata and lexical only serve as tie-breakers.
inter_pack_contradictions.claim_right: sourceWeights vector=0.92 (lowest); hardSignalVectorPenalty=-0.08 (further down-weights vector under hard signals)
inter_pack_contradictions.actual_truth: policy.ts is the authoritative on-disk policy. Vector is the WEAKEST source weight (0.92 vs worktree 1.3, metadata 1.15) and is further penalized under hard signals. The memory item is wrong.

inter_pack_contradictions.claim_left: Always prefer the top vector hit.
inter_pack_contradictions.claim_right: if (candidate.source === 'vector') { weight += policy.hardSignalVectorPenalty; } — implementation actively de-prioritizes vector when files/symbols/errors are present
inter_pack_contradictions.actual_truth: Implementation explicitly de-emphasizes vector under hard signals; the memory's prescription is inverted relative to the live code.

inter_pack_contradictions.claim_left: Vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa retrieval
inter_pack_contradictions.claim_right: Applies source weights: metadata strongest ... vector (last in ordered list)
inter_pack_contradictions.actual_truth: Docs and code agree vector is last. Memory contradicts both.

child_fact_check.claim: Pack lists k-fusion-source-weight and k-policy-default as essential, with two noise/contradictory items in optional
child_fact_check.evidence: Pack JSON sections.essential[] contains those two ids; sections.optional[] contains the two flagged items. Verified directly.

child_fact_check.claim: Lines 141-153 implement: if hasHardSignal, add hardSignalBoost.bonus to listed sources and add hardSignalVectorPenalty (-0.08) for vector candidates. Pack snippet matches exactly.
child_fact_check.evidence: Lines 141-153 of fusion.ts match the pack snippet verbatim. Confirmed.

child_fact_check.claim: sourceWeights vector=0.92 (weakest), worktree=1.3 (strongest); hardSignalBoost.bonus=0.18 on ['metadata','lexical','graph','worktree']; hardSignalVectorPenalty=-0.08
child_fact_check.evidence: policy.ts lines 184-196 confirmed. However the child stops here and never picks up the taskProfiles layer (lines 222-253) — see counter-context below.

child_fact_check.claim: Doc states 'metadata strongest ... vector' and 'Weighted below exact metadata and lexical matches when files, symbols, or errors are present' — consistent with the code.
child_fact_check.evidence: Confirmed in docs/FLOW_LOGIC.md lines 170, 174-180. Note: the two quoted fragments are from non-adjacent paragraphs (Vector search section vs. Fusion section) — child accepted the pack's stitched quote without flagging it.

child_fact_check.claim: Item claims 'vector search is the strongest signal in Tuberosa' but policy.ts shows vector=0.92 is the WEAKEST source weight. The memory directly contradicts the code on disk.
child_fact_check.evidence: Correct identification of the contradiction. Child handled this well.

child_fact_check.claim: PaywallSelectionModal is from a newsletter app and has no relationship to retrieval fusion — pure topical drift via vector match.
child_fact_check.evidence: Confirmed — the file does not exist in the tuberosa repo. Correct noise classification, though child did not verify the file is missing on disk (which would have strengthened the verdict).

child_fact_check.claim: answer field: 'while still keeping vector in the candidate pool (weight 0.92 - 0.08 = 0.84) for cases where no exact match exists'
child_fact_check.evidence: Math ignores taskProfiles. For the classified taskType 'exploration' (which the pack itself declares in classified.taskType), policy.ts line 244-247 adds +0.04 to vector via effectiveSourceWeight. The true effective vector weight under hard signals + exploration is 0.92 + 0.04 - 0.08 = 0.88, not 0.84. The child also did not explain that the additive delta layer (effectiveSourceWeight, line 394) sits between the base sourceWeights and the hard-signal penalty.

child_fact_check.claim: answer field: 'The asymmetric design (boost the precise sources AND penalize vector) widens the gap'
child_fact_check.evidence: Reasonable inference from the code, but not a citation — it is the child's own architectural commentary. Not a hallucination, but should be acknowledged as commentary rather than evidence.

missed_facts: taskProfiles layer in policy.ts (lines 222-253) — the exploration profile adds +0.04 to vector, materially changing the arithmetic the child presents. This is a first-class part of how the effective vector weight is computed.

effectiveSourceWeight() definition at policy.ts line 394 — this is the function actually called inside sourceWeight() (fusion.ts line 143), not a direct lookup of policy.sourceWeights[source]. The child's narrative collapses this layer.

RRF combination formula in fusion.ts line 55: contribution = sourceBoost / (rrfK + max(1, rank)). The penalty is multiplied through a rank-based divisor, so the magnitude of -0.08 depends on rank position — relevant to 'why is this design correct'.

test/fusion-profiles.test.ts — the verification command the pack itself recommended (orientation.verificationCommands). Neither pack content nor child output references what that test actually asserts.

policy.suppressionEnabled (lines 210-216) — explicitly enables stale/feedback/superseded/evidenceMismatch suppression. The fact that the contradictory memory slipped through optional despite this is the most interesting failure to surface, but it appears nowhere in pack or child.
alternative_queries_tried: grep -n 'hardSignalVectorPenalty\|hardSignalBoost' src/retrieval/*.ts — found 8 hits across policy.ts and fusion.ts; confirms the feature is localized to these two files
grep -n 'fuseCandidates' src/retrieval/fusion.ts — found 3 overload signatures at lines 31/35/40 (not in pack)
find test -name '*fusion*' — found test/fusion-profiles.test.ts (matches the pack's verificationCommands suggestion but its content was not surfaced)
should_have_been_essential: policy.ts taskProfiles.exploration block (lines 244-247) — directly relevant to the arithmetic for THIS query, since classified.taskType='exploration'
policy.ts effectiveSourceWeight() at line 394 — the function that wires base weights to task profiles; the actual entry point that sourceWeight() uses
fusion.ts fuseCandidates() RRF math (lines 51-55) — shows how sourceBoost combines with rank divisor; required to explain why -0.08 is the 'right' magnitude rather than arbitrary
test/fusion-profiles.test.ts — the pack's own recommended verification command; should at minimum have appeared as supporting

tuberosa_failure_modes: stale_memory_not_suppressed: k-old-stale-fusion-memory contradicts the live policy.ts but was kept in optional rather than rejected; suppressionEnabled.stale=true and .superseded=true should have caught this

noise_in_optional_section: k-paywall-modal-noise has zero domain overlap and references a path that does not exist in the repo; domainMismatch suppression (enabled=true, penalty=-0.3) should have dropped it
contradiction_undetected_at_retrieval: pack contains two items that directly contradict each other (k-policy-default vs k-old-stale-fusion-memory) and no inter-item contradiction flag was raised

stitched_quote_passed_as_verbatim: k-flow-logic-fusion content concatenates fragments from two non-adjacent paragraphs (line 170 + lines 174-180) but presents them as a single quoted block with a single line citation (line 173)
off_by_two_line_citation: k-flow-logic-fusion cites line 173 but actual content starts at line 174 (line 173 is blank); k-policy-default cites line 184 but the snippet spans 184-196
missing_taskProfile_layer: pack omits taskProfiles entirely, which causes the child to publish incorrect arithmetic (0.92 - 0.08 = 0.84) when the actual exploration-task effective weight is 0.88

truncated_essential_snippet: k-fusion-source-weight uses '...' to elide lines 155-162 of sourceWeight() which apply task-itemType boosts — these are part of the same function and relevant to its full behavior
verification_command_not_grounded: orientation.verificationCommands recommends a test file but its contents/assertions are never surfaced in any pack item
unverified_file_reference: k-paywall-modal-noise.references[0].uri points to a non-existent file with no warning
